name: node-path-is-inside
title: node-path-is-inside
summary: Tests whether one path is inside another path
description:
Tests whether one path is inside another path
The **path-is-inside** package will give you a robust, cross-platform
way of detecting whether a given path is inside another path.
.
Like Node's built-in path module, path-is-inside treats all file paths on
Windows as case-insensitive, whereas it treats all file paths on
*-nix operating systems as case-sensitive. Keep this in mind especially
when working on a Mac, where, despite Node's defaults, the OS usually treats
paths case-insensitively.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-path-is-inside because Tests whether one path is inside another path.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-path-is-inside as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-path-is-inside with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-path-is-inside --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
